comments_1: Ilene Gordon advised Thomas Malkoski of Ingredion's interest in acquiring Penford.
comments_2: No specific price was cited; Gordon referenced Penford's approximately $16.00 per share 52-week high and approximately $12.00 per share current trading price.

comments_1: Secrecy Agreement effective as of this date.
comments_2: Provided for limited exchange of confidential information and confidentiality of discussions.

comments_1: Oral indication; Fortnum suggested $17.00 per share on a fully diluted basis could likely get support from Ingredion's board.
comments_2: Malkoski responded that the price would probably not be high enough for Penford's board.

comments_1: Letter confirming interest in a possible business combination.
comments_2: Indicative valuation of $18.00 per share in cash for all outstanding capital stock on a fully diluted basis.

comments_1: Party A's Chief Executive Officer informally discussed Party A's potential interest in acquiring or combining with Penford.
comments_2: Party A said it saw value in Penford only at a typical takeover premium based on current trading price.

comments_1: Hatfield contacted Behrens to determine whether SEACOR was interested in acquiring the Company.
Behrens stated that SEACOR was not interested in participating in any potential sale process at that time.
Nondisclosure and standstill agreement executed on this date.
comments_2: Superseded the prior Secrecy Agreement.

comments_1: Board authorized management and Deutsche Bank to proceed with a market check process while continuing negotiations and due diligence with Ingredion.
After Deutsche Bank's outreach in the market check, Party A expressed interest in having further discussions regarding a transaction.
comments_2: Deutsche Bank forwarded Party A a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.

comments_1: Party B expressed interest in further discussions after being contacted by Deutsche Bank.
comments_2: Deutsche Bank forwarded Party B a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.

comments_1: Party C expressed interest in pursuing a transaction.
Legal advisor: Perkins Coie
comments_2: Board had directed management on 07/24/2014 to retain Deutsche Bank; engagement letter was executed on this date.

comments_1: Party D expressed interest after being contacted by Deutsche Bank.
comments_2: Deutsche Bank forwarded a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.

comments_1: Party B informed Deutsche Bank that it had decided not to move forward with discussions or sign a nondisclosure agreement.
Deutsche Bank left a voicemail for Party E regarding a potential transaction.
comments_2: In the following two weeks Deutsche Bank left several additional voicemails and received no response.

comments_1: Executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
comments_2: Penford then provided Party C information regarding strategy, business segments, technologies, end markets, manufacturing, historical financial performance and projections.

comments_1: Fortnum reported that Ingredion could potentially increase its proposal from $18.00 per share to $18.25 or $18.50 per share on a fully diluted basis.
comments_2: By this point the parties had discussed the status of a draft merger agreement and J.P. Morgan had sent Deutsche Bank an initial draft on 09/06/2014, but the background does not say Ingredion had received a marked-up draft from Penford as of this call.

comments_1: Executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
Deutsche Bank contacted Party F regarding a potential transaction with Penford.
Party F communicated that a combination with Penford was not a suitable strategic fit and declined further discussions.
Executed a nondisclosure and standstill agreement.
comments_2: Deutsche Bank told Party A that Penford would not provide in-person management presentations unless Party A's proposed offer price would likely be over $20.00 per share.

comments_1: Fortnum stated that Ingredion was prepared to move forward based on a proposed price of $18.50 per share on a fully diluted basis.
comments_2: Malkoski responded that the price was unlikely to be high enough for Penford's board to recommend the transaction.
comments_3: The background reflects ongoing discussion of a draft merger agreement, but it does not state that Ingredion had received a marked-up draft from Penford as of this October 2 call.

comments_1: Ingredion letter increased the proposed price to $19.00 per share of outstanding Penford capital stock on a fully diluted basis.
comments_2: Revised indication of interest.
comments_3: The filing describes this as a revised indication of interest and does not state that Ingredion had received a marked-up draft merger agreement before this letter.

comments_1: After reviewing Ingredion's revised $19.00 indication and the market-check update, the board unanimously directed management to proceed to negotiate and finalize a definitive agreement with Ingredion.
comments_2: Later that day, Malkoski informed Fortnum that the board had authorized management to move forward with negotiating the draft merger agreement.

comments_1: Party A indicated any offer would be below $17.50 to $18.00 per share in cash.
comments_2: Deutsche Bank encouraged Party A to submit a letter of interest for board review.

comments_1: Party D indicated that it did not intend to move forward with discussions regarding a potential transaction involving the Company.
Party A indicated that its value range had been reduced from $17.50 to $18.00 per share to $16.00 to $18.00 per share.
comments_2: Party A cited increased volatility in the markets.

comments_1: Fortnum confirmed the proposed price of $19.00 and discussed finalizing the merger agreement.
comments_2: Ingredion maintained this price after reviewing Penford's draft fiscal 2014 financial statements.
comments_3: Also on October 14, Perkins Coie and Sidley Austin exchanged successive drafts of the merger agreement and disclosure letter and discussed the updated drafts.

comments_1: Party A delivered a formal letter indicating interest at $16.00 per share.
comments_2: Party A cited increased volatility in the markets as the reason for the decrease in price.

comments_1: At the board meeting, Deutsche Bank reported that among the remaining NDA parties one had not moved forward in a timely manner; this appears to refer to Party C.
Penford and Ingredion finalized and executed the merger agreement during the evening.
comments_2: Penford, Ingredion and SEACOR also executed the voting and support agreement.
